clinical_history: This ECG from a 49-year-old man shows which of the following?
diagnosis: b) Pure left anterior fascicular block (or hemiblock) causes marked left axis deviation (-45 degrees to -90 degrees),  typically with rS complexes in II, III, and aVF, and qR complexes  in I and aVL, with a QRS duration of <120 ms.  (RS or Rs complexes  may be seen in the left chest leads with left anterior fascicular block.) Rosenbaum MB et al.,  The Hemiblocks.  Oldsmar, FL: Tampa Tracings, 1970.
